output.summary: Four changes to the Header/HeaderMain block: (1) rename toggle label, (2) add editor spaceholder when overlay mode is active, (3) change pt-8 to mt-8 on outer wrapper, (4) fix frontend overlay mode by adding relative positioning to outer wrapper. Changes affect TSX, PHP, and JS files. No attribute schema changes needed.

fileChanges.description: Rename toggle label and add editor spaceholder for overlay mode, change pt-8 to mt-8
fileChanges.details: 1. Line 382: Change ToggleControl label from 'Display above content (overlay mode)' to 'Display over the content (overlay mode)'. 2. Line 455: Change 'flex flex-col gap-2 pt-8' to 'flex flex-col gap-2 mt-8'. 3. Add 'relative' to the outer wrapper className (always-on) so that the absolute-positioned .header-main positions relative to it. 4. After the existing header-main-spacer div (line 702), add a spaceholder div that is only visible in the editor when displayAboveContent is true. This div should have a height matching the header to push the block appender/button bar below the header. Use a style like { height: 'var(--header-main-height, 120px)' } and only show it when displayAboveContent is true. Alternatively, since --header-main-height is not set in the editor, use a useEffect + ResizeObserver on the .header-main element inside the iframe to measure and set a CSS variable or state value for the spaceholder height.

fileChanges.description: Change pt-8 to mt-8 and add relative positioning to outer wrapper
fileChanges.details: 1. Line 42: Change 'flex flex-col gap-2 pt-8' to 'relative flex flex-col gap-2 mt-8'. The 'relative' class ensures the absolute-positioned .header-main child stays within the outer wrapper bounds. The mt-8 change moves the spacing from padding (which occupies space inside the block and pushes the absolute child down) to margin (which is outside the block's visual area). 2. The negative margin class '-mb-[var(--header-main-height,0px)]' remains unchanged as it is the mechanism for overlay mode.

fileChanges.description: Ensure --header-main-height is set early enough for the negative margin to work on initial render
fileChanges.details: The JS already calls updateHeaderHeight() during initialization (line 339) which sets --header-main-height on document.documentElement. However, the CSS class -mb-[var(--header-main-height,0px)] is on the block wrapper element, not on :root. Verify the CSS variable cascades correctly. The current implementation sets it on document.documentElement which should cascade to all descendants. If the variable resolves to 0px on first paint (before JS runs), there will be a brief flash where overlay mode doesn't work. Consider: (a) setting a reasonable CSS fallback value instead of 0px (e.g., 120px as a typical header height), or (b) adding the --header-main-height inline style to the outer wrapper in PHP as well. Option (b) is preferred: in HeaderMain.php, compute the approximate header height server-side or use a known CSS variable to set an initial fallback so the negative margin works before JS runs. Alternatively, accept the brief flash since JS runs almost immediately on page load.

designDecisions.decision: Change pt-8 (padding-top) to mt-8 (margin-top) on the HeaderMain outer wrapper
designDecisions.evidence: User requested: 'The space between the header and the top of the screen (currently using padding) should use margin instead, so it doesn't occupy space in the block'. Padding inside the block pushes the absolute-positioned .header-main child down from the top edge. Margin on the outer wrapper creates the same visual spacing without affecting the absolute child's position within the block.
designDecisions.impact: The header bar will now align flush with the top of the outer wrapper. The mt-8 margin creates space between the header block and whatever is above it (e.g., admin bar or page top). This also means the SEP Mobile Button (which is a sibling before .header-main) will be inside the wrapper but above the header bar, positioned at the top of the block without the extra padding offset.

designDecisions.decision: Add 'relative' positioning to the outer wrapper when overlay mode is used
designDecisions.evidence: The .header-main div uses 'absolute left-0 right-0 z-200' when displayAboveContent is true. Without a 'relative' ancestor on the direct parent wrapper, the absolute positioning references a higher ancestor (possibly the page container), causing the header to escape its intended bounds. Adding 'relative' to the outer wrapper ensures the header positions within its own block boundaries.
designDecisions.impact: The absolute-positioned .header-main will now be contained within the block wrapper. Combined with the negative bottom margin, this creates the correct overlay behavior where the header visually sits on top of the content below it.

designDecisions.decision: Add a spaceholder div in the editor to maintain block appender position below the header when overlay mode is enabled
designDecisions.evidence: User reported: 'when overlay mode is enabled, the button bar stays on top of the header since it isn't considering the actual header height'. The negative bottom margin collapses the block's visual height, causing Gutenberg's block toolbar/appender to overlap the header content.
designDecisions.impact: A spaceholder div will be rendered after the header content in the editor only. It will match the header's rendered height to push the block insertion UI below the visible header area. This div is editor-only and does not affect the frontend.

designDecisions.decision: Rename toggle label from 'Display above content' to 'Display over the content'
designDecisions.evidence: User explicitly requested: 'Rename Display above content to Display over the content'
designDecisions.impact: Label-only change in the editor UI. No attribute rename needed since the attribute name 'displayAboveContent' is an internal identifier that does not need to match the label exactly.

qaFocus: Verify label reads 'Display over the content (overlay mode)' in the Behaviour panel
Verify mt-8 spacing appears correctly on frontend (top margin above header)
Verify overlay mode works on frontend: header overlays the content below, content is not pushed down
Verify the editor shows a spaceholder that prevents the block appender from overlapping the header in overlay mode
Verify non-overlay mode still works correctly (header in normal flow, no negative margin)
Verify fixed-on-scroll behavior still works in both overlay and non-overlay modes
Verify mobile menu still opens/closes correctly in overlay mode

technicalNotes.rootCause: The frontend overlay mode has two issues: (1) The outer wrapper lacks 'relative' positioning, so the 'absolute' .header-main escapes its parent bounds and positions relative to a distant ancestor. (2) The 'pt-8' padding creates 32px of space inside the wrapper above the absolute child, pushing it down from the expected position. These combine to make the overlay mode ineffective on the frontend.
technicalNotes.editorSpaceholder: In the editor, --header-main-height is not set by JS (the frontend JS is not loaded in the editor). The spaceholder height should either use a fixed estimate or be calculated via a useEffect that measures the .header-main element height within the editor iframe. A pragmatic approach: use a ref on the .header-main div and a ResizeObserver to track its height, then apply that height to the spaceholder div. This ensures the spaceholder accurately reflects the actual rendered header height in the editor.
technicalNotes.cssVariableFallback: The -mb-[var(--header-main-height,0px)] class uses 0px as a fallback. On initial frontend page render before JS sets the variable, the negative margin is 0 and the overlay effect won't work. Consider changing the fallback to a reasonable estimate (e.g., 120px) or setting the variable server-side in PHP as an inline style. However, since the JS runs immediately on DOMContentLoaded, the flash is minimal and likely acceptable.
